title: Man convicted more than 3 decades after raping 2 women at gunpoint in Framingham store
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, was convicted for raping two women at gunpoint in 1989 in a Framingham store.
Gale was identified as a suspect in 2024 through modern DNA technology and investigative genetic genealogy.
Gale evaded police for decades, using multiple identities and led a car chase before his arrest in Los Angeles.

title: New Providence basketball coach goes viral for recruiting joke: ‘Just look at my wife’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bryan Hodgson introduced as Providence basketball head coach, facing rebuilding challenges after two sub-.500 seasons.
Hodgson's joke about recruiting prowess referencing his wife Jordan went viral during his introductory press conference.
Hodgson led South Florida to a 25-9 record and an NCAA tournament bid in the 2025-26 season.

title: He’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children in foster care
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alex Castrichini, 37, from Concord, Mass., will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support children in foster care.
Castrichini's involvement with Boston CASA aims to raise awareness about foster children's challenges and need for supportive adults.
Inspired by a personal story shared at a Boston CASA Gala, Castrichini joined the board and now runs for awareness.

title: ‘Children’s lives are at stake’: Boston teachers protest staffing cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Over 400 Boston Public School jobs could be cut, affecting teachers and support staff for high-needs students.
Protestors, including the Boston Teachers Union, gathered outside City Hall advocating for increased investment in schools.
The proposed budget cuts coincide with school closures and reconfigurations due to declining enrollment.

title: Where ‘No Kings’ protests are happening around Massachusetts this Saturday
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Over 160 'No Kings' protests will occur in Massachusetts on March 28, aligned with nationwide demonstrations against various political issues.
Massachusetts ranks second in protest events after California, with over 3,000 protests planned across the United States.
Protests are a response to issues like immigration policies and U.S. involvement in Iran, organized by Mass 50501.

title: ESPN’s ‘Pardon The Interruption’ hosts believe Jaylen Brown should win NBA MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN's 'Pardon The Interruption' hosts Wilbon and Kornheiser support Jaylen Brown for NBA MVP this season.
Jaylen Brown is averaging career highs with 28.5 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.1 assists per game through 64 games.
Despite Jayson Tatum's injury, the Celtics hold second in the Eastern Conference with a 47-24 record.

title: Marshfield police searching for missing 8-year-old boy
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Marshfield police are searching for 8-year-old Callahan Pierce, last seen Tuesday near 236 Moraine St. around 7 p.m.
Pierce was wearing a black sweatshirt and sweatpants and was carrying a backpack, according to Marshfield Police Department.
Residents are asked to check home cameras and contact police at 781-834-6655 with any information about the missing child.

title: Will James Hagens play for the Bruins this season? Don Sweeney responds.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens signed an AHL amateur tryout agreement with the Providence Bruins, starting his professional career Wednesday.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney stated the team won't rush Hagens into Boston's lineup without him acclimating to the pro game.
Hagens, having excelled at Boston College, is viewed as a potential late-season boost, but NHL play isn't guaranteed this season.

title: Man convicted, sentenced to life in prison 40 years after teenager found dead in Plymouth forest
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Hand, 69, sentenced to life for 1986 murder of 15-year-old Tracy Gilpin in Plymouth.
Tracy Gilpin's body was found with a massive skull fracture in Myles Standish State Park in 1986.
Hand's statements during 2018 interviews linked him to the crime scene and led to his conviction.

title: Pedestrian struck and killed on Mass. Pike
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was fatally struck while crossing I-90 East near the 54-mile marker in Ludlow, Massachusetts.
The incident occurred Monday around 12:30 p.m., prompting a response from State Police and local emergency services.
The victim was transported to a hospital and pronounced dead; their identity was not immediately released.
